featured_videos.title: This Is The New Butter (And You’ll Be FORCED To Eat It!)
featured_videos.creator: Steak and Butter Gal
featured_videos.editorial_title: This Is The New Butter (And You’ll Be FORCED To Eat It!)
featured_videos.commentary: Okay, so the community is rightfully fired up about this one. What's striking isn't just the butter itself, it's the pattern people are noticing: ingredients changing quietly, labeling getting fuzzier, and regulatory bodies that seem more interested in protecting corporate interests than consumers. That's hitting a nerve because carnivores are already hyperaware of what's in their food, and when your diet is built on whole foods, additives feel like a betrayal. The farm-to-table comments aren't nostalgia, they're a wake-up call that local sourcing might actually become necessary again, not trendy. This is exactly why so many people in the community are reconnecting with local farmers and asking harder questions about their protein sources.

featured_videos.title: I'm Eating This ENTIRE Pizza Everyday and Losing Weight
featured_videos.creator: Steak and Butter Gal
featured_videos.editorial_title: I'm Eating This ENTIRE Pizza Everyday and Losing Weight
featured_videos.commentary: The comments here are telling the real story. Someone reversed heart disease and lost 60 pounds. Another person ditched daily Tylenol for headaches. These aren't small wins, and they're not accidents. What Steak and Butter Gal is showing is that eating carnivore doesn't mean deprivation. it means swapping the foods that were hurting you for versions that actually satisfy. The cottage cheese crust idea is genius because it solves the biggest psychological barrier people face when they start. You don't have to white-knuckle your way through cravings or convince yourself steamed chicken is a treat. You get actual pizza, the texture you're craving, the satisfaction you remember. That's why this works where most diets fail, and why the weight loss becomes almost inevitable when the food stops fighting against you.
